name: Yodd
author: Luis Bolaños Mures / AGP port
description: Yodd, by Luis Bolaños Mures. On an empty hexhex board, players alternate placing one or two stones of EITHER colour (Red moves first, and only one stone on the opening turn). A group is a set of connected like-coloured stones, and at the end of every turn the TOTAL number of groups on the board must be odd. You may pass when it keeps the count odd; two passes in a row end the game, and whoever has FEWER groups of their own colour wins. Because the total is odd, the two counts can never tie — there are no draws. Boards of side 6–9 are recommended.
